en.extract:
The island leaf warbler (Phylloscopus poliocephalus) is a species of Old World warbler in the family Phylloscopidae.
It is found in an area ranging from the Moluccas to the Solomon Islands.


Taxonomy
The island leaf warbler was formally described in 1876 as Gerygone? poliocephala by the Italian zoologist Tommaso Salvadori based on a specimen collected in the Arfak Mountains of western New Guinea. The specific epithet combines the Ancient Greek πολιος/polios meaning "grey" or "grizzled" with -κεφαλος/-kephalos meaning "-headed". The island leaf warbler is now one of around 80 leaf warblers placed in the genus  Phylloscopus that was introduced in 1826 by the German zoologist Friedrich Boie.
Eighteen subspecies are recognised:

P. p. suaramerdu Rheindt, FE, Prawiradilaga, DM, Ashari, H, Suparno & Ng, NSR, 2020 – montane Peleng, in Banggai Island (off eastern Sulawesi) (Peleng leaf warbler)
P. p. emilsalimi Rheindt, FE, Prawiradilaga, DM, Ashari, H, Suparno & Ng, NSR, 2020 – montane Taliabu (Sula Islands) (Taliabu leaf warbler)
P. p. henrietta Stresemann, EFT, 1931 – northern Moluccas (Halmahera and Ternate)
P. p. waterstradti (Hartert, EJO, 1903) – montane Moluccas (Bacan Islands and Obi Islands)
P. p. everetti (Hartert, EJO, 1899) – montane Buru (southern Moluccas)
P. p. ceramensis (Ogilvie-Grant, WR, 1910) – montane southern Moluccas (Seram and Ambon)
P. p. avicola Hartert, EJO, 1924 – montane Kai Besar Island (Kai Islands)
P. p. matthiae Rothschild, LW & Hartert, EJO, 1924 – Mussau Island, in St. Matthias Group (north-central Bismarck Archipelago)
P. p. moorhousei Gilliard, ET & LeCroy, MK, 1967 – montane New Britain and Umboi Island (southeastern Bismarck Archipelago)
P. p. leletensis Salomonsen, F, 1965 – montane New Ireland (northeastern Bismarck Archipelago)
P. p. poliocephalus (Salvadori, AT, 1876) – montane northwestern New Guinea (Tamrau, Arfak, and Wandammen mountains)
P. p. albigularis Hartert, EJO & Paludan, KJ, 1936 – montane west-central New Guinea
P. p. cyclopum Hartert, EJO, 1930 – montane northern New Guinea (Cyclops Mountains)
P. p. giulianettii (Salvadori, AT, 1896) – montane New Guinea (Snow, Sepik, Saruwaged, and Herzog mountains)
P. p. hamlini Mayr, E & Rand, AL, 1935 – Goodenough Island, in D'Entrecasteaux Archipelago (off southeastern New Guinea)
P. p. becki Hartert, EJO, 1929 – montane Guadalcanal, Santa Isabel, and Malaita (central Solomon Islands)
P. p. bougainvillei Mayr, E, 1935 – montane Bougainville Island (northwestern Solomon Islands)
P. p. pallescens Mayr, E, 1935 – montane Kolombangara (west-central Solomon Islands)

The '''island leaf warbler''' ('''''Phylloscopus poliocephalus''''') is a species of [[Old World warbler]] in the family [[Phylloscopidae]].
It is found in an area ranging from the [[Moluccas]] to the [[Solomon Islands]].

==Taxonomy==
The island leaf warbler was [[species description|formally described]] in 1876 as ''Gerygone? poliocephala'' by the Italian zoologist [[Tommaso Salvadori]] based on a specimen collected in the [[Arfak Mountains]] of western New Guinea.<ref>{{ cite journal | last=Salvadori | first=Tommaso | author-link=Tommaso Salvadori | date=1875 | publication-date=1876 | title=Descrizione di cinquantotto nuove specie di uccelli, ed osservazioni intorno ad altre poco note, della Nuova Guinea e di altre Isole Papuane, raccolte dal Dr. Opoarpo Reccari e dai cacciatori del Sig. A.A. Bruijn | language=Italian, Latin | journal=Annali del Museo Civico di Storia Naturale di Genova | volume=7 | pages=896–976 [960] | url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/34621409 }}</ref><ref>{{ cite book | editor1-last=Mayr | editor1-first=Ernst | editor1-link=Ernst Mayr | editor2-last=Cottrell | editor2-first=G. William | year=1986 | title=Check-list of Birds of the World | volume=11 | publisher=Museum of Comparative Zoology | place=Cambridge, Massachusetts | page=254 | url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/14483955 }}</ref> The specific epithet combines the [[Ancient Greek]] πολιος/''polios'' meaning "grey" or "grizzled" with -κεφαλος/''-kephalos'' meaning "-headed".<ref>{{ cite web | last=Jobling | first=James A. | title=poliocephalus | work=The Key to Scientific Names | url=https://birdsoftheworld.org/bow/key-to-scientific-names/search?q=poliocephalus | publisher=Cornell Lab of Ornithology | access-date=3 August 2025 }}</ref> The island leaf warbler is now one of around 80 leaf warblers placed in the genus  ''Phylloscopus'' that was introduced in 1826 by the German zoologist [[Friedrich Boie]].<ref name=avilist>{{ cite web | author=AviList Core Team | date=2025 | title=AviList: The Global Avian Checklist, v2025 | doi=10.2173/avilist.v2025 | doi-access=free | url=http://www.avilist.org/checklist/v2025/ | access-date=28 November 2025 }}</ref>

Eighteen [[subspecies]] are recognised:<ref name=avilist/>
* ''P. p. suaramerdu'' Rheindt, FE, Prawiradilaga, DM, Ashari, H, Suparno & Ng, NSR, 2020 – montane Peleng, in [[Banggai Island]] (off eastern [[Sulawesi]]) (Peleng leaf warbler)<ref name=rheindt>{{Cite journal | last1=Rheindt | first1=F.E. | last2=Prawiradilaga | first2=D.M. | last3=Ashari | first3=H. | last4=Suparno | last5=Gwee | first5=C.Y. | last6=Lee | first6=G.W.X. | last7=Wu | first7=M.Y. | last8=Ng | first8=N.S.R. | date=2020 | title=A lost world in Wallacea: description of a montane archipelagic avifauna | journal=Science | volume=367 | issue=6474 | pages=167-170 | doi=10.1126/science.aax2146}} See supplement.</ref>
* ''P. p. emilsalimi'' Rheindt, FE, Prawiradilaga, DM, Ashari, H, Suparno & Ng, NSR, 2020 – montane Taliabu ([[Sula Islands Regency|Sula Islands]]) (Taliabu leaf warbler)<ref name=rheindt/>
* ''P. p. henrietta'' [[Erwin Stresemann|Stresemann, EFT]], 1931 – northern [[Maluku Islands|Moluccas]] ([[Halmahera]] and [[Ternate]])
* ''P. p. waterstradti'' ([[Ernst Hartert|Hartert, EJO]], 1903) – montane [[Maluku Islands|Moluccas]] ([[Bacan Islands]] and [[Obi Islands]])
* ''P. p. everetti'' (Hartert, EJO, 1899) – montane [[Buru]] (southern [[Maluku Islands|Moluccas]])
* ''P. p. ceramensis'' ([[William Robert Ogilvie-Grant|Ogilvie-Grant, WR]], 1910) – montane southern [[Maluku Islands|Moluccas]] ([[Seram Island|Seram]] and [[Ambon Island|Ambon]])
* ''P. p. avicola'' Hartert, EJO, 1924 – montane Kai Besar Island ([[Kai Islands]])
* ''P. p. matthiae'' [[Walter Rothschild, 2nd Baron Rothschild|Rothschild, LW]] & Hartert, EJO, 1924 – [[Mussau Island]], in St. Matthias Group (north-central [[Bismarck Archipelago]])
* ''P. p. moorhousei'' [[Ernest Thomas Gilliard|Gilliard, ET]] & LeCroy, MK, 1967 – montane [[New Britain]] and [[Umboi Island]] (southeastern [[Bismarck Archipelago]])
* ''P. p. leletensis'' [[Finn Salomonsen|Salomonsen, F]], 1965 – montane [[New Ireland (island)|New Ireland]] (northeastern [[Bismarck Archipelago]])
* ''P. p. poliocephalus'' ([[Tommaso Salvadori|Salvadori, AT]], 1876) – montane northwestern [[New Guinea]] (Tamrau, Arfak, and Wandammen mountains)
* ''P. p. albigularis'' Hartert, EJO & [[Knud Paludan|Paludan, KJ]], 1936 – montane west-central [[New Guinea]]
* ''P. p. cyclopum'' Hartert, EJO, 1930 – montane northern [[New Guinea]] ([[Cyclops Mountains]])
* ''P. p. giulianettii'' (Salvadori, AT, 1896) – montane [[New Guinea]] (Snow, Sepik, Saruwaged, and Herzog mountains)
* ''P. p. hamlini'' [[Ernst Mayr|Mayr, E]] & [[Austin L. Rand|Rand, AL]], 1935 – [[Goodenough Island]], in [[D'Entrecasteaux Islands|D'Entrecasteaux Archipelago]] (off southeastern [[New Guinea]])
* ''P. p. becki'' Hartert, EJO, 1929 – montane [[Guadalcanal]], [[Santa Isabel (island)|Santa Isabel]], and [[Malaita]] (central [[Solomon Islands]])
* ''P. p. bougainvillei'' Mayr, E, 1935 – montane [[Bougainville Island]] (northwestern [[Solomon Islands]])
* ''P. p. pallescens'' Mayr, E, 1935 – montane [[Kolombangara]] (west-central [[Solomon Islands]])
